title: Workflow — design cache layer from scratch for e-commerce product catalog
code_snippet:
# Goal: add Redis caching to product catalog service
# Current: all reads hit PostgreSQL directly, ~3000 QPS, p99 latency 150ms
# Target: reduce p99 to <20ms for product reads
# Products updated ~100 times/day via admin panel
# 50K products total, top 1000 products get 80% of traffic
expected_feedback: Workflow: cache-aside is the right pattern for this scenario (read-heavy 3000 QPS, ~100 writes/day, 30s staleness OK). Design: (1) Key schema: product:{id}, TTL 30min with jitter. (2) Singleflight for top-1000 hot products to prevent stampede. (3) Null-value caching for non-existent product IDs (penetration defense). (4) On product update via admin panel: invalidate cache key immediately. (5) Degradation: on Redis failure, fall through to PostgreSQL with circuit breaker. (6) Warmup: eager-load top-1000 products on deploy. (7) Monitor: cache hit rate target >95%, alert if <90%.
